name: Sweet and Sour Peppers
recipeIngredient: 1 tsp Thai hot sauce, chili oil, or other hot sauce (optional)
3 Scallions, whites minced, greens thinly sliced diagonally
2 Red bell peppers, cored, seeded, and cut into 1 1/2-inch squares
2 Yellow bell peppers, cored, seeded, and cut into 1 1/2-inch squares
2 Green bell peppers, cored, seeded, and cut into 1 1/2-inch squares
1 tbsp Black or toasted white sesame seeds
1 1/2 tsp Cornstarch dissolved in 1 tbsp Cold water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, whisk together the lemon juice, tamari, honey, and hot sauce (if using).
2. Heat a wok over high heat and swirl in the oil.
3. Add the garlic, ginger, chili, and scallion whites. Stir-fry for 15 seconds or until fragrant but not browned.
4. Add the peppers and stir-fry for 1 minute or until they begin to soften.
5. Stir in half the scallion greens, half the sesame seeds, and the sauce. Bring to a boil.
6. Stir in the dissolved cornstarch and cook for about 30 seconds.
7. Transfer to a platter and garnish with the remaining scallion greens and sesame seeds.
